report.title: Global Renewal Atlas & Stewardship Roadmap (2026–2050+)
report.abstract: This report details the Global Renewal Atlas, the technical architecture for managing the multi-decade lifecycle of planetary AI governance. It specifies the stewardship roadmap, the governance metabolism model, and the decision-discipline required to navigate deep uncertainty during the transition from AGI to ASI.
report.content:
# 1. Global Renewal Atlas: Technical Architecture
## 1.1 The "Renewal Engine" The Renewal Atlas is a distributed state-machine that manages the evolution of the Civilizational Covenant across 5-year "First-Cycle" implementation windows. - **Reference Implementation**: A federated cluster of **Sentinel v3.0** nodes, each hosting a local shard of the global alignment state. - **Pilot Scenarios**: Testing "Partial Compliance" and "Regional Drift" within simulation environments before committing state-updates to the Atlas.
## 1.2 Governance Metabolism Model The "Metabolism" defines the rate at which the governance stack can ingest new capability data and re-align its safety policies. - **Fast-Loop (Reactive)**: Real-time SEV-0 kill-switch activation and OPA-based blocking. - **Slow-Loop (Adaptive)**: Quarterly "Constitutional Review" by the Global Stewardship Council to update the GDL baseline.
---
# 2. Decision-Discipline Under Uncertainty
## 2.1 Navigating the ASI Horizon As system capability exceeds human interpretability, governance must rely on **Formal Verification of Probabilistic Kernels**. - **Self-Correcting Governance**: The stack utilizes "Alignment Detectors" that monitor for semantic divergence. If detected, the system automatically reverts to the last known "Safe Attractor" state. - **Terminal Closure**: The architectural state where meaning, action, and legitimacy are aligned, and the governance metabolism reaches a "Terminal Attractor" of stable human-AI symbiosis.
